text.title: Agentic RAG — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `agentic-rag` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Graph state is a Pydantic model; all transitions read/write typed fields. Untyped dict state is rejected.
rule.rationale: Untyped state in LangGraph drifts; typed state catches transition bugs at graph compile time.

rule.statement: First node is a query router that picks (vectorstore | web | reject). Skipping the router and always retrieving wastes budget.
rule.rationale: Domain queries hit the vectorstore; trivia queries hit web; «what's today's weather» is rejected — only the router chooses correctly.

rule.statement: Retrieved docs MUST be graded for relevance BEFORE generation. Generating on irrelevant docs is the #1 hallucination source.
rule.rationale: Relevance grader + generation costs ~25% extra tokens but cuts hallucination ~3x in production.

rule.statement: Max 3 retry hops; on third failure return a degraded answer + uncertainty flag, not infinite recursion.
rule.rationale: Unbounded loops on hard queries blow latency budgets and never converge; bounded loops degrade gracefully.

rule.statement: Final answer passes a groundedness grader (does answer cite the retrieved docs?). Ungrounded answers route back to retrieval.
rule.rationale: Groundedness gate catches model confabulation that the relevance grader missed; it's the last line of defence before user-visible answer.
